## PR Title
Fix resuming after `ds.set_epoch(new_epoch)`

## PR Body
close https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/issues/7447

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
Model-Architecture-Scope-Change-Action (MASCA) focused on this PR

- Model (what is changing)
  - Behavior: Fix how IterableDataset (examples iterables) handle loading a saved state dict after calling set_epoch(new_epoch), so the epoch-driven RNG state isn't unintentionally overwritten and resuming works as expected.

- Architecture (where in the code)
  - The iterable dataset machinery lives in src/datasets/iterable_dataset.py. There is a base examples iterable class (_BaseExamplesIterable) that implements state dict loading logic used by all concrete examples iterable classes (shuffled iterables, randomly cycling iterables, etc.).
  - The bug is in the state loading logic: when load_state_dict() is called with a state that contains None or partial structure, the existing in-memory _state_dict can be replaced with None (or be overwritten unexpectedly), which can unintentionally revert generator state set by set_epoch(new_epoch).

- Scope (what to change exactly)
  - Modify _BaseExamplesIterable.load_state_dict to avoid overwriting existing nested state with None when new_state is None. Instead, if a subtree in new_state is None, keep the existing subtree in state. This makes load_state_dict act as a merge that preserves existing entries unless explicitly provided in new_state.
  - This small, targeted change preserves runtime changes (like set_epoch updating RNG state in iterables) when load_state_dict is used to restore other parts of the iteration state.

- Change (how to implement)
  - In _BaseExamplesIterable.load_state_dict's inner recursive helper, change the fallback return from `return new_state` to `return state if new_state is None else new_state`. This ensures that when new_state is None we keep the current state subtree instead of overwriting it with None.

- Tests / Validation to add
  - Add a unit test that:
    1) Creates an IterableDataset with shuffling (so that there is a generator / bit_generator_state stored in the iterable state),
    2) Advances/consumes some elements, grabs state_dict(), then calls set_epoch(new_epoch) (which updates the generator state),
    3) Calls load_state_dict(saved_state) to restore the iteration state but while preserving the newly set epoch, and asserts that iteration resumes with the epoch-based RNG behavior (e.g., different shard order than original epoch 0) rather than being reverted to the old RNG state.

Rationale summary: the minimal fix is to make load_state_dict merge (preserve) existing state when new_state contains None subtrees, preventing set_epoch-updated RNG state from being overwritten when loading other parts of the state.

## Files to Modify
- src/datasets/iterable_dataset.py: Contains the _BaseExamplesIterable base class used by all examples iterables. The issue is due to load_state_dict behavior in this base class which can overwrite current in-memory state (including RNG/epoch state) with None when a partial state is loaded. Fixing load_state_dict here fixes behavior across all iterable dataset types.

## Functions to Modify
- _BaseExamplesIterable.load_state_dict (src/datasets/iterable_dataset.py): This method's recursive helper currently returns new_state even when new_state is None, which results in replacing existing in-memory sub-state with None. Change the fallback so that when new_state is None the existing state subtree is preserved (i.e., return state when new_state is None). This prevents load_state_dict from clobbering an RNG/epoch state that was set by ds.set_epoch(new_epoch).

## Analysis Summary
Problem summary
- Users call ds.set_epoch(new_epoch) to change RNG/epoch used for shuffling in iterable datasets. Later when they try to resume by loading a previously saved state dict, the internal load_state_dict implementation can overwrite parts of the current in-memory _state_dict (including RNG/bit_generator state) with None or old values from the saved state. This makes the dataset resume with the wrong RNG state (losing the effect of set_epoch), breaking expected resuming behavior.

Root cause
- The recursive helper inside _BaseExamplesIterable.load_state_dict returns new_state unconditionally at the end of the function. When new_state is None for a subtree, this replaces the existing state subtree with None. That means partial loads or loads that don't include RNG-related fields will still wipe existing RNG state that was set previously (e.g., by set_epoch).

Proposed minimal fix
- Modify _BaseExamplesIterable.load_state_dict (in src/datasets/iterable_dataset.py) so that the recursion preserves existing state when new_state is None (return state when new_state is None, otherwise return new_state). This turns load_state_dict into a merge that keeps currently present sub-states unless explicitly overwritten in new_state.

Why this fixes ds.set_epoch(new_epoch)
- Calls to set_epoch typically update RNG-related fields inside the in-memory _state_dict. After the fix, subsequent load_state_dict calls that don't intend to overwrite RNG state will no longer clobber those RNG fields, so resuming after set_epoch works as expected.

Files/functions to change
- src/datasets/iterable_dataset.py: _BaseExamplesIterable.load_state_dict — change single-line fallback return to preserve state when new_state is None.

Suggested tests
- Add a unit test that demonstrates the workflow: set_epoch -> save/modify state -> load saved state -> ensure RNG/epoch from set_epoch is preserved and iteration resumes correctly.

This is a small, low-risk localized change (single method) affecting all iterable dataset variants that rely on the base class for state loading. It fixes the reported issue about resuming after set_epoch without touching broader shuffling or generator logic.

steps.side_effects: Behavioral: Calls to load_state_dict that include None in some subtrees will no longer wipe the corresponding existing in-memory subtrees; instead those subtrees will be preserved. This fixes resuming after ds.set_epoch(new_epoch) for all iterable dataset variants that inherit from _BaseExamplesIterable (shuffle/cycle/etc.). Tests: Existing tests that relied on the old behavior of replacing subtrees with None may need to be updated; add (or update) a unit test that verifies resuming after set_epoch(new_epoch) (advance dataset, save state, set_epoch, load saved state, ensure RNG/epoch-driven behavior is preserved). Dependents/API: No public API change; change is internal to iterable dataset state handling, but it changes semantics of load_state_dict (from overwrite-with-None to preserve-on-None). Backwards compatibility: likely safe and desirable, but audit callers that intentionally expected None-to-clobber behavior. Performance: negligible extra branching in the recursive helper.
